title: California Invests Over $835 Million in Green Transportation Projects to Drive Emissions Reduction through Cap-and-Invest Program
content:
Governor Gavin Newsom today announced more than $865 million in new investments â€” funded entirely by big polluters â€” to build affordable housing, expand transit, and protect communities from climate change. The awards include funding for 39 new affordable housing in 21 communities across the state, thousands of new homes, and major sustainable transportation upgrades, with $185.6 million going to Los Angeles County as it rebuilds infrastructure after this yearâ€™s devastating wildfires.
The California Strategic Growth Council (SGC) today surpassed a historic $5 billion investment milestone after awarding nearly $1 billion â€” the councilâ€™s largest award announcement â€” to affordable housing, community resilience, and agricultural conservation projects. These investments:

Fund affordable housing and transportation projects close to jobs, schools, and other daily destinations.
Build climate resilience through protecting our productive farmlands and encouraging compact transit-oriented communities.
Support community-led climate solutions that achieve major environmental, health, and economic benefits in Californiaâ€™s most disadvantaged communities.
â€œThis milestone reflects our commitment to keeping communities safe and healthy for the long haul,â€ said SGC Executive Director Erin Curtis. â€œThese investments will help families breathe cleaner air, stay protected during extreme heat, find stable places to live and work, and preserve the lands that feed us. Crossing the $5 billion mark is about people-centered resilience and showing real partnership with the neighborhoods hit first and worse by climate impacts. We are so proud to be a part of that!â€

The funding approved today by the Strategic Growth Council, included more than $866 million in grant funding for 39 projects within three programs:

Affordable Housing and Sustainable Communities Program â€“ $835,318,208 for 21 affordable housing and green transportation projects in 17 jurisdictions through Round 9 grants
Transformative Climate Communities Program â€” $29,484,224 from the anticipated fiscal year 2026-2027 Proposition 4 (Climate Bond) to fully fund four Round 5 grants
Sustainable Agricultural Lands Conservation Program â€”- $2,051,490 for 14 capacity-building projects in 25 counties through Round 10 grants
author: Analysts Account
